Interim Project — Supply Chain Demand Prediction (Baseline)¶

Learner: Barnabas R
Project: Predict optimum quantity to ship to each warehouse (product_wg_ton)
Date: 2026-02-22

Business Context & Objective¶

An FMCG company needs to minimize stockouts and waste by optimizing shipments to warehouses. The objective is to build a predictive model (baseline: Linear Regression) to forecast product_wg_ton per warehouse and provide insights for targeted actions (advertising, redistribution, safety stock).

In [29]:
# ==========================================
# 1. UNDERSTANDING THE BUSINESS PROBLEM
# ==========================================
"""
Problem Statement:
The goal is to predict the product weight ('product_wg_ton') handled by warehouses based on
logistical, infrastructure, and operational parameters.

Need of the project:
Inaccurate capacity forecasting leads to stockouts or excessive overhead. By predicting
tonnage accurately, the management can optimize labor, logistics, and supply chain efficiency.

Business Opportunity:
Implementing this model allows for data-driven decisions regarding warehouse expansion,
resource allocation, and risk management (e.g., flood-proofing in specific zones).
"""

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split, GridSearchCV
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler, LabelEncoder
from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression
from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeRegressor
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestRegressor, BaggingRegressor, AdaBoostRegressor, GradientBoostingRegressor
from sklearn.metrics import mean_squared_error, r2_score, mean_absolute_error

# Plotting setup
sns.set_theme(style="whitegrid")
plt.rcParams['figure.figsize'] = (10, 6)

# Helpers
import matplotlib.ticker as mtick

def show_hist(col, bins=40, kde=True):
    plt.figure(figsize=(7,4))
    sns.histplot(df[col].dropna(), bins=bins, kde=kde)
    plt.title(f"Histogram: {col}")
    plt.xlabel(col)
    plt.show()

def show_box(col):
    plt.figure(figsize=(7,3))
    sns.boxplot(x=df[col].dropna())
    plt.title(f"Boxplot: {col}")
    plt.show()

def safe_to_numeric(series):
    return pd.to_numeric(series.astype(str).str.replace(',','').str.strip(), errors='coerce')

# quick conversion attempt for likely numeric columns (no assignment yet)
likely_num = ["WH_capacity_size","dist_from_hub","wh_est_year","num_refill_req_l3m",
              "transport_issue_l1y","Competitor_in_mkt","retail_shop_num","distributor_num",
              "wh_breakdown_l3m","govt_check_l3m","workers_num","product_wg_ton"]
print("Helpers ready.")

Helpers ready.
In [35]:
#   Numeric univariate deep dive
num_cols = df.select_dtypes(include=[np.number]).columns.tolist()
# ensure target not duplicated
if "product_wg_ton" in num_cols:
    num_cols.remove("product_wg_ton")

summary = []
for c in num_cols:
    s = df[c]
    n_nonnull = s.dropna().shape[0]
    mean = s.mean()
    median = s.median()
    std = s.std()
    skew = s.skew() if n_nonnull>0 else np.nan
    kurt = s.kurtosis() if n_nonnull>0 else np.nan
    p1 = s.quantile(0.01)
    p5 = s.quantile(0.05)
    p95 = s.quantile(0.95)
    p99 = s.quantile(0.99)
    summary.append((c, n_nonnull, mean, median, std, skew, kurt, p1, p5, p95, p99))

num_summary = pd.DataFrame(summary, columns=["feature","non_null","mean","median","std","skew","kurtosis","p1","p5","p95","p99"])
display(num_summary.sort_values("non_null", ascending=False).head(40))

# show histograms for top 6 numeric features by variance
top6 = df[num_cols].var().sort_values(ascending=False).index[:6].tolist()
for col in top6:
    show_hist(col, bins=50, kde=True)
    show_box(col)

In [38]:
#   Bivariate numeric vs target
# choose numeric features with enough non-null
candidate_num = [c for c in df.columns if pd.api.types.is_numeric_dtype(df[c]) and c != target and df[c].dropna().shape[0] > len(df)*0.3]
# take top 8 by variance
candidate_num = pd.Series(candidate_num).tolist()
candidate_num = df[candidate_num].var().sort_values(ascending=False).index[:8].tolist()

for c in candidate_num:
    plt.figure(figsize=(7,4))
    sns.scatterplot(x=df[c], y=df[target], alpha=0.35, s=6)
    # add lowess smooth if seaborn has it
    try:
        sns.regplot(x=df[c], y=df[target], scatter=False, lowess=True, line_kws={'color':'red'})
    except Exception:
        pass
    plt.title(f"{target} vs {c}")
    plt.show()

#   Missingness exploration
plt.figure(figsize=(12,4))
sns.heatmap(df.isnull().astype(int).T, cbar=False)
plt.title("Missingness heatmap (cols x rows) - large display")
plt.show()

# Missingness percent
miss_pct = (df.isnull().sum() / len(df) * 100).sort_values(ascending=False)
display(miss_pct.head(40))

# Check if missingness of key cols correlates with target (informative missingness)
interesting = miss_pct[miss_pct>0].index.tolist()[:15]
for c in interesting:
    mflag = df[c].isnull().astype(int)
    corr_with_target = mflag.corr(df[target], method='pearson')
    if pd.notna(corr_with_target) and abs(corr_with_target) > 0.05:
        print(f"Missingness in {c} correlates with target: {corr_with_target:.3f}")

In [43]:
#Outliers identification
outlier_flags = {}
for c in [target] + num_cols[:10]:
    series = df[c].dropna()
    if len(series) < 10:
        continue
    q1 = series.quantile(0.25); q3 = series.quantile(0.75)
    iqr = q3 - q1
    lower = q1 - 3*iqr
    upper = q3 + 3*iqr
    flag = (df[c] < lower) | (df[c] > upper)
    outlier_count = flag.sum()
    outlier_flags[c] = outlier_count
    if outlier_count>0:
        print(f"{c}: extreme outliers (IQR-based) = {outlier_count}")
# percent-based extreme
pct_upper = df[target].quantile(0.999)
print(f"{target} 99.9th percentile: {pct_upper:.3f}")

In [44]:
#   Feature engineering examples
df_fe = df.copy()

# wh_age
if "wh_est_year" in df_fe.columns:
    df_fe["wh_est_year"] = safe_to_numeric(df_fe["wh_est_year"])
    df_fe["wh_age"] = 2026 - df_fe["wh_est_year"]

# capacity utilization proxy
if "WH_capacity_size" in df_fe.columns:
    df_fe["WH_capacity_size"] = safe_to_numeric(df_fe["WH_capacity_size"])
    df_fe["cap_util_proxy"] = df_fe[target] / df_fe["WH_capacity_size"].replace({0:np.nan})

# refill per month
if "num_refill_req_l3m" in df_fe.columns:
    df_fe["refills_per_month"] = safe_to_numeric(df_fe["num_refill_req_l3m"]) / 3

# competitor per shop
if set(["Competitor_in_mkt","retail_shop_num"]).issubset(df_fe.columns):
    df_fe["Competitor_in_mkt"] = safe_to_numeric(df_fe["Competitor_in_mkt"])
    df_fe["retail_shop_num"] = safe_to_numeric(df_fe["retail_shop_num"])
    df_fe["comp_per_shop"] = df_fe["Competitor_in_mkt"] / df_fe["retail_shop_num"].replace({0:np.nan})

# dist_bin (if dist exists)
if "dist_from_hub" in df_fe.columns:
    df_fe["dist_from_hub"] = safe_to_numeric(df_fe["dist_from_hub"])
    df_fe["dist_bin"] = pd.cut(df_fe["dist_from_hub"].fillna(-1), bins=[-1,10,50,200,1000,1e9], labels=["unknown_near","near","short","mid","far"])

# risk_index from binary flags
risk_cols = []
for c in ["transport_issue_l1y","flood_impacted","storage_issue_reported_l3m","wh_breakdown_l3m"]:
    if c in df_fe.columns:
        df_fe[c+"_bin"] = df_fe[c].apply(lambda x: 1 if str(x).strip().lower() not in ["0","false","nan","none",""] else 0)
        risk_cols.append(c+"_bin")
if risk_cols:
    df_fe["risk_index"] = df_fe[risk_cols].sum(axis=1)

# preview engineered features
preview_cols = [c for c in ["wh_age","cap_util_proxy","refills_per_month","comp_per_shop","dist_bin","risk_index"] if c in df_fe.columns]
display(df_fe[preview_cols].head())
wh_age cap_util_proxy refills_per_month comp_per_shop dist_bin risk_index
0 NaN NaN 1.000000 0.000430 short 3
1 NaN NaN 0.000000 0.000643 mid 2
2 NaN NaN 0.333333 0.000929 short 2
3 NaN NaN 2.333333 0.000333 short 3
4 17.0 NaN 1.000000 0.000422 short 4
In [45]:
 #Leakage heuristics
leakage = []
for c in df_fe.columns:
    if c == target:
        continue
    # exact equality suspicious
    try:
        eq = (df_fe[c] == df_fe[target]).sum()
        if eq > 0:
            leakage.append((c, f"{eq} exact equals"))
    except Exception:
        pass
    # extremely high correlation
    if pd.api.types.is_numeric_dtype(df_fe[c]):
        try:
            corr = df_fe[[c,target]].dropna().corr().iloc[0,1]
            if pd.notna(corr) and abs(corr) > 0.995:
                leakage.append((c, f"corr={corr:.3f}"))
        except Exception:
            pass
display({"leakage_warnings": leakage})

{'leakage_warnings': [('retail_shop_num', '1 exact equals')]}

In [47]:
# Prepare data, feature engineering, and group-aware split (idempotent)
import os, warnings
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")
import numpy as np, pandas as pd
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split

# Ensure DATA_PATH is correctly set to the Google Drive path
DATA_PATH = '/content/drive/My Drive/supply_chain_data.csv'

df = pd.read_csv(DATA_PATH)
TARGET = "product_wg_ton"
GROUP_COL = "Ware_house_ID"

# Basic FE
df_fe = df.copy()
# coerce numeric-ish columns safely
num_like = ["WH_capacity_size","dist_from_hub","wh_est_year","num_refill_req_l3m",
            "transport_issue_l1y","Competitor_in_mkt","retail_shop_num","distributor_num",
            "wh_breakdown_l3m","govt_check_l3m","workers_num","product_wg_ton"]
for c in num_like:
    if c in df_fe.columns:
        df_fe[c] = pd.to_numeric(df_fe[c].astype(str).str.replace(',','').str.strip(), errors='coerce')

# Feature engineering
if "wh_est_year" in df_fe.columns:
    df_fe["wh_age"] = 2026 - df_fe["wh_est_year"]
if "WH_capacity_size" in df_fe.columns:
    df_fe["cap_util_proxy"] = df_fe[TARGET] / df_fe["WH_capacity_size"].replace({0:np.nan})
if "num_refill_req_l3m" in df_fe.columns:
    df_fe["refills_per_month"] = df_fe["num_refill_req_l3m"] / 3
if set(["Competitor_in_mkt","retail_shop_num"]).issubset(df_fe.columns):
    df_fe["comp_per_shop"] = df_fe["Competitor_in_mkt"] / df_fe["retail_shop_num"].replace({0:np.nan})
if "dist_from_hub" in df_fe.columns:
    df_fe["dist_bin"] = pd.cut(df_fe["dist_from_hub"].fillna(-1), bins=[-1,10,50,200,1000,1e9], labels=["unknown_near","near","short","mid","far"])

# risk index
risk_cols = []
for c in ["transport_issue_l1y","flood_impacted","storage_issue_reported_l3m","wh_breakdown_l3m"]:
    if c in df_fe.columns:
        df_fe[c+"_bin"] = df_fe[c].apply(lambda x: 1 if str(x).strip().lower() not in ["0","false","nan","none",""] else 0)
        risk_cols.append(c+"_bin")
if risk_cols:
    df_fe["risk_index"] = df_fe[risk_cols].sum(axis=1)

# define features
identifier_cols = [GROUP_COL, "WH_Manager_ID"] if "WH_Manager_ID" in df_fe.columns else ([GROUP_COL] if GROUP_COL in df_fe.columns else [])
features = [c for c in df_fe.columns if c != TARGET and c not in identifier_cols and c not in ['index','Unnamed: 0']]
# split numeric/categorical
numeric_features = [c for c in features if pd.api.types.is_numeric_dtype(df_fe[c])]
categorical_features = [c for c in features if c not in numeric_features]

# quick checks
print("Rows,Cols:", df_fe.shape)
print("Numeric features:", len(numeric_features), "| Categorical features:", len(categorical_features))

# group-aware split
RANDOM_STATE = 42
USE_GROUP_SPLIT = True
if USE_GROUP_SPLIT and GROUP_COL in df_fe.columns and df_fe[GROUP_COL].nunique()>1:
    uniq = df_fe[GROUP_COL].dropna().unique()
    g_train, g_test = train_test_split(uniq, test_size=0.2, random_state=RANDOM_STATE)
    train_idx = df_fe[df_fe[GROUP_COL].isin(g_train)].index
    test_idx = df_fe[df_fe[GROUP_COL].isin(g_test)].index
    X_train = df_fe.loc[train_idx, features].reset_index(drop=True)
    X_test  = df_fe.loc[test_idx, features].reset_index(drop=True)
    y_train = df_fe.loc[train_idx, TARGET].reset_index(drop=True)
    y_test  = df_fe.loc[test_idx, TARGET].reset_index(drop=True)
    split_type = "group"
else:
    X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(df_fe[features], df_fe[TARGET], test_size=0.2, random_state=RANDOM_STATE)
    split_type = "random"

print("Split:", split_type, "| Train rows:", len(X_train), "Test rows:", len(X_test))

Rows,Cols: (25000, 34)
Numeric features: 25 | Categorical features: 6
Split: group | Train rows: 20000 Test rows: 5000
In [48]:
# Preprocessing checks + EDA summary
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt, seaborn as sns
sns.set_style("whitegrid")
import numpy as np

# Duplicate check
dup_count = int(df_fe.duplicated().sum())
print("Duplicate rows:", dup_count)

# Missingness summary (top 12)
miss_pct = (df_fe.isnull().sum() / len(df_fe) * 100).sort_values(ascending=False)
display(miss_pct.head(12))

# Outlier checks for target
if TARGET in df_fe.columns:
    desc = df_fe[TARGET].describe(percentiles=[0.01,0.05,0.25,0.5,0.75,0.95,0.99]).to_dict()
    print("Target describe (sample):")
    display(pd.Series(desc))

# simple outlier flags (IQR)
outlier_report = {}
for c in numeric_features:
    s = df_fe[c].dropna()
    if len(s) < 10:
        continue
    q1, q3 = s.quantile(0.25), s.quantile(0.75)
    iqr = q3 - q1
    lower, upper = q1 - 3*iqr, q3 + 3*iqr
    outlier_report[c] = int(((df_fe[c] < lower) | (df_fe[c] > upper)).sum())
# show top numeric outlier counts
outlier_df = pd.Series(outlier_report).sort_values(ascending=False).head(10)
display(outlier_df)

# Plot: target distribution + boxplot
plt.figure(figsize=(10,4))
plt.subplot(1,2,1)
sns.histplot(df_fe[TARGET].dropna(), bins=80, kde=True)
plt.title("Target distribution (product_wg_ton)")
plt.subplot(1,2,2)
sns.boxplot(x=df_fe[TARGET].dropna())
plt.title("Target boxplot")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# Bivariate example: target vs retail_shop_num and vs dist_from_hub (if exist)
if "retail_shop_num" in df_fe.columns:
    plt.figure(figsize=(6,3))
    sns.scatterplot(x=df_fe["retail_shop_num"], y=df_fe[TARGET], alpha=0.35, s=8)
    plt.title("product_wg_ton vs retail_shop_num")
    plt.show()
if "dist_from_hub" in df_fe.columns:
    plt.figure(figsize=(6,3))
    sns.scatterplot(x=df_fe["dist_from_hub"], y=df_fe[TARGET], alpha=0.35, s=8)
    plt.title("product_wg_ton vs dist_from_hub")
    plt.show()

# Multivariate: correlation top by abs corr with target
num_all = df_fe.select_dtypes(include=[np.number]).columns.tolist()
if TARGET in num_all:
    num_all.remove(TARGET)
    corr_abs = df_fe[num_all + [TARGET]].corr()[TARGET].abs().sort_values(ascending=False)
    display(corr_abs.head(12))
    top_corr_feats = corr_abs.head(12).index.tolist()
    plt.figure(figsize=(8,6))
    sns.heatmap(df_fe[top_corr_feats + [TARGET]].corr(), annot=True, fmt=".2f", cmap="vlag")
    plt.title("Correlation heatmap (top features)")
    plt.show()

print("EDA checks complete. Copy the displayed plots and tables into your report (univariate/bivariate/multivariate).")

In [49]:
# Preprocessing pipeline (impute/scale/ohe) and baseline Linear Regression
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler, OneHotEncoder
from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer
from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression
from sklearn.metrics import mean_squared_error, mean_absolute_error, r2_score
import math, numpy as np

num_pipeline = Pipeline([("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="median")), ("scaler", StandardScaler())])
_ohe_kwargs = {}
try:
    OneHotEncoder(sparse_output=False)
    _ohe_kwargs["sparse_output"] = False
except Exception:
    _ohe_kwargs["sparse"] = False
cat_pipeline = Pipeline([("impute", SimpleImputer(strategy="most_frequent")), ("ohe", OneHotEncoder(handle_unknown="ignore", **_ohe_kwargs))])
preprocessor = ColumnTransformer([("num", num_pipeline, numeric_features), ("cat", cat_pipeline, categorical_features)])

def eval_preds(y_true, y_pred):
    mask = ~(np.isnan(y_true) | np.isnan(y_pred))
    y_true = np.asarray(y_true)[mask].astype(float)
    y_pred = np.asarray(y_pred)[mask].astype(float)
    mse = mean_squared_error(y_true, y_pred); rmse = math.sqrt(mse)
    mae = mean_absolute_error(y_true, y_pred); r2 = r2_score(y_true, y_pred)
    mape = np.mean(np.abs((y_true - y_pred) / np.where(y_true==0, np.nan, y_true))) * 100
    return {"rmse": rmse, "mae": mae, "r2": r2, "mape": mape}

# Baseline Linear Regression
lr_pipe = Pipeline([("pre", preprocessor), ("model", LinearRegression())])
lr_pipe.fit(X_train, y_train)
y_pred_lr = lr_pipe.predict(X_test)
metrics_lr = eval_preds(y_test, y_pred_lr)
print("Baseline Linear Regression metrics (test):")
print(metrics_lr)

Baseline Linear Regression metrics (test):
{'rmse': 1127.5276752387972, 'mae': 874.9430932152577, 'r2': 0.9904983557076125, 'mape': np.float64(6.067788219205324)}
In [28]:
# Train at least 5 advanced models and compare test metrics
from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeRegressor
from sklearn.ensemble import BaggingRegressor, RandomForestRegressor, AdaBoostRegressor, GradientBoostingRegressor
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
import pandas as pd

models = {
    "DecisionTree": DecisionTreeRegressor(random_state=RANDOM_STATE),
    "Bagging": BaggingRegressor(n_estimators=100, random_state=RANDOM_STATE),
    "RandomForest": RandomForestRegressor(n_estimators=200, n_jobs=-1, random_state=RANDOM_STATE),
    "AdaBoost": AdaBoostRegressor(n_estimators=100, random_state=RANDOM_STATE),
    "GradientBoosting": GradientBoostingRegressor(n_estimators=200, random_state=RANDOM_STATE)
}
# try XGBoost
use_xgb = False
try:
    from xgboost import XGBRegressor
    models["XGBoost"] = XGBRegressor(n_estimators=200, random_state=RANDOM_STATE, n_jobs=-1, verbosity=0)
    use_xgb = True
except Exception:
    pass

results = {}
for name, model in models.items():
    pipe = Pipeline([("pre", preprocessor), ("model", model)])
    pipe.fit(X_train, y_train)
    yhat = pipe.predict(X_test)
    m = eval_preds(y_test, yhat)
    results[name] = {"pipe": pipe, "metrics": m}
    print(f"{name:12} | RMSE: {m['rmse']:.3f} | MAE: {m['mae']:.3f} | R2: {m['r2']:.4f} | MAPE: {m['mape']:.2f}%")

# Show table sorted by RMSE
res_df = pd.DataFrame({k: v["metrics"] for k,v in results.items()}).T.sort_values("rmse")
display(res_df)

In [50]:
# Hyperparameter tuning for 3+ models (limited n_iter for speed)
from sklearn.model_selection import RandomizedSearchCV

tune_candidates = {}
# RandomForest
tune_candidates["RandomForest"] = {
    "estimator": Pipeline([("pre", preprocessor), ("model", RandomForestRegressor(random_state=RANDOM_STATE))]),
    "params": {
        "model__n_estimators": [100,200,400],
        "model__max_depth": [8,12,20,None],
        "model__min_samples_leaf": [1,2,5],
        "model__max_features": ["sqrt", 0.3, 0.5]
    }
}
# GradientBoosting
tune_candidates["GradientBoosting"] = {
    "estimator": Pipeline([("pre", preprocessor), ("model", GradientBoostingRegressor(random_state=RANDOM_STATE))]),
    "params": {
        "model__n_estimators": [100,200,400],
        "model__learning_rate": [0.01,0.05,0.1],
        "model__max_depth": [3,5,8],
        "model__min_samples_leaf": [1,2,5]
    }
}
# XGBoost
from sklearn.ensemble import AdaBoostRegressor
if use_xgb:
    from xgboost import XGBRegressor
    tune_candidates["XGBoost"] = {
        "estimator": Pipeline([("pre", preprocessor), ("model", XGBRegressor(random_state=RANDOM_STATE, verbosity=0))]),
        "params": {
            "model__n_estimators": [100,200,400],
            "model__learning_rate": [0.01,0.05,0.1],
            "model__max_depth": [3,5,8],
            "model__subsample": [0.6,0.8,1.0]
        }
    }
else:
    tune_candidates["AdaBoost"] = {
        "estimator": Pipeline([("pre", preprocessor), ("model", AdaBoostRegressor(random_state=RANDOM_STATE))]),
        "params": {
            "model__n_estimators": [50,100,200],
            "model__learning_rate": [0.01,0.05,0.1,0.2]
        }
    }

tuned_results = {}
for name, cfg in tune_candidates.items():
    print(f"Tuning {name} ...")
    rs = RandomizedSearchCV(cfg["estimator"], cfg["params"], n_iter=8, cv=3, scoring="neg_root_mean_squared_error", random_state=RANDOM_STATE, n_jobs=-1, verbose=0)
    rs.fit(X_train, y_train)
    best = rs.best_estimator_
    yhat = best.predict(X_test)
    m = eval_preds(y_test, yhat)
    tuned_results[name] = {"best_estimator": best, "best_params": rs.best_params_, "test_metrics": m, "cv_score": rs.best_score_}
    print(f" -> {name} test RMSE: {m['rmse']:.3f} | MAE: {m['mae']:.3f} | R2: {m['r2']:.4f} | MAPE: {m['mape']:.2f}%")

Tuning RandomForest ...
 -> RandomForest test RMSE: 874.204 | MAE: 648.021 | R2: 0.9943 | MAPE: 4.07%
Tuning GradientBoosting ...
 -> GradientBoosting test RMSE: 849.287 | MAE: 635.656 | R2: 0.9946 | MAPE: 4.00%
Tuning XGBoost ...
 -> XGBoost test RMSE: 858.285 | MAE: 639.865 | R2: 0.9945 | MAPE: 4.08%
In [51]:
# Compare tuned models and pick final by lowest test RMSE
import pandas as pd
tune_summary = []
for name, info in tuned_results.items():
    t = info["test_metrics"]
    tune_summary.append((name, t["rmse"], t["mae"], t["r2"], t["mape"]))
tune_df = pd.DataFrame(tune_summary, columns=["model","rmse","mae","r2","mape"]).sort_values("rmse")
display(tune_df)

best_name = tune_df.iloc[0]["model"]
best_info = tuned_results[best_name]
best_model = best_info["best_estimator"]
best_metrics = best_info["test_metrics"]
print(f"Selected final model: {best_name}")
print("Final test metrics:", best_metrics)

In [52]:
# Robust permutation importance + safe feature-name alignment
from sklearn.inspection import permutation_importance
import pandas as pd, numpy as np, warnings

# compute permutation importances (may take time)
print("Computing permutation importance (this can take ~20-60s depending on size)...")
perm = permutation_importance(best_model, X_test, y_test, n_repeats=8, random_state=42, n_jobs=-1)

# attempt to get correct feature names after preprocessing
def safe_get_transformed_feature_names(preprocessor, original_feature_list):
    # 1) try get_feature_names_out (sklearn >=1.0)
    try:
        if hasattr(preprocessor, "get_feature_names_out"):
            names = list(preprocessor.get_feature_names_out())
            print("Using preprocessor.get_feature_names_out()")
            return names
    except Exception as e:
        # fall through to other methods
        pass

    # 2) try ColumnTransformer.fit_transformer inspection (best-effort)
    try:
        names = []
        for name, transformer, cols in preprocessor.transformers_:
            if name == "remainder":
                continue
            # pipeline last step
            trans = transformer
            if hasattr(transformer, "named_steps"):
                trans = transformer.named_steps[list(transformer.named_steps.keys())[-1]]
            # OneHotEncoder case
            if hasattr(trans, "categories_"):
                # cols might be a list; handle both single and list
                if isinstance(cols, (list, tuple)):
                    cols_list = list(cols)
                else:
                    cols_list = [cols]
                for col_name, categories in zip(cols_list, trans.categories_):
                    for cat in categories:
                        names.append(f"{col_name}__{cat}")
            else:
                # passthrough numeric columns
                if isinstance(cols, (list, tuple)):
                    names.extend(list(cols))
                else:
                    names.append(cols)
        if len(names)>0:
            print("Using best-effort expansion from preprocessor.transformers_")
            return names
    except Exception as e:
        pass

    # 3) fallback: return original features (will likely be shorter than transformed)
    print("Falling back to original feature list (may be shorter than transformed features).")
    return list(original_feature_list)

# call helper
try:
    pre = best_model.named_steps['pre']
except Exception:
    # if best_model is not a pipeline but a raw estimator trained on transformed arrays:
    pre = preprocessor  # use the notebook-level preprocessor object as fallback

feat_names = safe_get_transformed_feature_names(pre, features)

# Compare lengths and handle mismatch
n_feat_names = len(feat_names)
n_importances = len(perm.importances_mean)
print(f"Num feat names: {n_feat_names} | Num importances: {n_importances}")

if n_feat_names != n_importances:
    warnings.warn(
        "Feature-name length does not match number of importances. "
        "We'll align safely by truncating or padding generated names. "
        "Investigate preprocessor.get_feature_names_out() availability if you want exact original labels.",
        UserWarning
    )
    # if feat_names shorter -> pad with placeholders
    if n_feat_names < n_importances:
        pad_len = n_importances - n_feat_names
        feat_names = feat_names + [f"unknown_f_{i}" for i in range(pad_len)]
    # if feat_names longer -> truncate
    elif n_feat_names > n_importances:
        feat_names = feat_names[:n_importances]

# Build DataFrame safely
imp_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "feature": feat_names,
    "imp_mean": perm.importances_mean,
    "imp_std": perm.importances_std
})
imp_df = imp_df.sort_values("imp_mean", ascending=False).reset_index(drop=True)
display(imp_df.head(40))

# If model has tree-based importances, show those too (align similarly)
try:
    tree = best_model.named_steps['model']
    if hasattr(tree, "feature_importances_"):
        raw_imp = tree.feature_importances_
        # ensure same length alignment
        if len(feat_names) != len(raw_imp):
            print("Warning: tree.feature_importances_ length differs from feat_names length. Aligning by truncation/pad.")
            if len(feat_names) < len(raw_imp):
                pad = len(raw_imp) - len(feat_names)
                feat_names_tree = feat_names + [f"unknown_f_{i}" for i in range(pad)]
            else:
                feat_names_tree = feat_names[:len(raw_imp)]
        else:
            feat_names_tree = feat_names
        raw_df = pd.DataFrame({"feature": feat_names_tree, "importance": raw_imp}).sort_values("importance", ascending=False).reset_index(drop=True)
        print("\nTree-based importances (top):")
        display(raw_df.head(30))
except Exception as e:
    print("Tree-based importances not available or failed:", e)

In [53]:
#Residual diagnostics and final metrics printout
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt, seaborn as sns
y_pred_final = best_model.predict(X_test)
resids = np.asarray(y_test).astype(float) - np.asarray(y_pred_final).astype(float)

plt.figure(figsize=(7,4))
plt.scatter(y_pred_final, resids, s=8, alpha=0.4)
plt.axhline(0, color='r', linestyle='--')
plt.xlabel("Predicted"); plt.ylabel("Residual"); plt.title(f"Residuals vs Predicted ({best_name})")
plt.show()

plt.figure(figsize=(6,3))
sns.histplot(resids[~np.isnan(resids)], bins=50, kde=True)
plt.title("Residual distribution (final model)")
plt.show()

print("Final model metrics:")
for k,v in best_metrics.items():
    if isinstance(v, float):
        print(f" - {k.upper():4}: {v:.4f}")
    else:
        print(f" - {k}: {v}")

Final model metrics:
 - RMSE: 849.2875
 - MAE : 635.6563
 - R2  : 0.9946
 - MAPE: 3.9988
